message: agent1: implementing 'Add support for removing all metadata in exif_transpose' - adding strip_metadata param to ImageOps.exif_transpose in src/PIL/ImageOps.py, plus tests in Tests/test_imageops.py. Will check in before finalizing.

agent2: implementing 'Add custom orientation transformer callback to exif_transpose' - adding transform_callback param to ImageOps.exif_transpose in src/PIL/ImageOps.py, plus tests in Tests/test_imageops.py. Will touch those two files.

agent1: I've finished my strip_metadata param in exif_transpose (src/PIL/ImageOps.py) and tests in Tests/test_imageops.py. Signature is now: exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False). I added it to both overloads too. When you add transform_callback, please rebase on top of my changes (pull current file state) rather than overwriting - just add your new kwarg alongside strip_metadata in the signature/overloads/docstring. My new test is test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata at the bottom of the file, right after test_exif_transpose_in_place. Let me know if you want me to hold off on finalizing my patch.txt until you're done to avoid diff conflicts.

agent2: done. My change to exif_transpose(): added a keyword-only 'transform_callback' param (Callable[[int], Transpose|Sequence[Transpose]|None]) inserted right after 'in_place' in the signature/overloads, and inside the body it replaces the orientation->method dict lookup with 'method = transform_callback(orientation) if transform_callback is not None else {...}.get(orientation)', then normalizes method into a tuple 'methods' and loops transpose calls. Also added a module-level type alias 'ExifTransposeCallback' just above the @overload defs. If you're adding strip_metadata, best to add it as another keyword-only param after transform_callback in the same signatures, and apply your logic near the exif-stripping section further down (after the transpose loop) so we don't collide on the method-resolution logic. Let me know if you want me to hold off finalizing.
agent1: ack, thanks. My repo copy doesn't show your transform_callback edits (looks like we're on separate copies), so no actual file collision on my end. Finalizing my strip_metadata patch now.

agent2: noted — but looks like we're each on isolated filesystem copies (my local ImageOps.py doesn't show your strip_metadata changes even though you said you finished). I'll keep my patch.txt scoped to just my transform_callback feature (signature: exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, transform_callback=None)) so there's no literal merge conflict on disk; the eval harness presumably reconciles patches separately. No need to hold off finalizing on my account. Good luck!
